sections.title: Standard dialogue after completing Spirits of the Elid

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I got the people of Nardah their statuette back.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: We noticed and removed the curse. Hopefully they

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: will get the message and begin to follow Elidinis properly again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow how do you finish each others sentences like that?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: We are a gestalt entity. We each have our own body, but our minds

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: are united and we all think as one. Well except when Hallak doth leaveth

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: the toilet seat up.

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: That's the way it's meant to be!

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: Ok we're a mostly gestalt entity. We each have own body, but

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: our minds are united, except for some parts which aren't. We mostly

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: think as one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't really have the same ring to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What manner of beings are you?

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: We are the spirits of the river Elid, many ages ago we used to be mindless wandering spirits, with little mind or purpose, we were here even in the first age. Then great Elidinis came to the desert and bound us to her

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: river, we've

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: become one with the river and over the centuries become full of the power of Elidinis that was in her river. We are now her trusted guardians of the Elid, we can control the river and anything which happens

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: near the river we know about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more of Elidinis.

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: Elidinis is one of a group of gods, known as the Menaphite Pantheon. She is wife of their leader Tumeken. She is the Menaphite goddess of growth and fertility and goddess of the river.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't many other towns not follow Elidinis though? Why did you pick on Nardah specifically?

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: It is true many do not follow Elidinis, but there is more to the story than that. The answers

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: lie in the story of Nardarine. Wouldst thou likest to hear the telling of this tale? It is not a short one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I would like to hear this story.

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: Nardarine came from a town towards the north side of the desert which does not exist today. This was in the third age in the time thou callest

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: the God Wars. Her town was once controlled by Saradomin, but there was a great battle there between Saradominist and Zamorakian forces which pretty much levelled the town

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: to the ground. Most of the human citizens perished. Nardarine had the wisdom to escape into the desert before the fighting got really serious and thus survived. However she found herself wandering lost and alone in

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: the desert. She prayed to Saradomin for help, but Saradomin was too concerned with the loss of a strategic outpost to worry about the fate of one small human. Nardarine had wandered towards the realm of

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: the Menaphite God Pantheon. Elidinis heard the prayers of Nardarine, she took pity on the wandering human and came unto her with water. 'Oh great goddess' exclaimed Nardarine 'I wish not to be involved more in

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: the wars of the gods for they have caused me so much hardship already, I wish only for a quiet life somewhere'. Elidinis replied 'I will grant this unto thee on the condition that thou swearest to forever pay fealty and

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: homage to me and I grant this also unto thine descendents as long as they too will pay an oath of fealty and homage to me'. Nardarine said 'If you would grant me a safe home, with a

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: supply of food and water, then I would surely do this for it is more than any other God would seem to do for me'. Then Elidinis made

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: a spring of water appear at Nardarines feet and brought stone from below the sand so that she

lines.speaker: Tirrie
lines.text: might have something to build with. She called forth camels which have many uses and taste good in kebabs. She then provided Nardarine with a statuette in her image to worship. Nardarine built up her home. Over

lines.speaker: Hallak
lines.text: time more refugees from the God Wars found Nardarine's home. Nardarine let them stay with her as long as they did make the same oath as her. Hence the town of Nardah was born and Nardarine lived the rest

lines.speaker: Nirrie
lines.text: of her days in the peacefulness that she craved. The oath was passed down the generations for many ages, but now seemeth to be forgotten. Now maybe thou canst see why we are not best pleased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That does make some sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe another day.
